metadata.title:
G0LOF|ELO IS


UNDER GUNS

MINE OWNERS ADD PRIVATE
PATROL TO TROOPS AND
MAKE EVERY CITIZEN AC-
COUNT FOR HIMSELF.

pages.raw_text:
GOLDFIELD, Dec. 9.-When
dawn broke this morning Goldfield
had the appearance of a fortress
Every mine looked like an encamp.
ment of troops. During the night
the mine owners increased the
guard and patrolled the town Ev'
cry pedestrian was compelled to
stop and give an account of him
self.


This was resented by citizens,
but 1t did not deter the mine own.
ers' private guards from persist
ing in the patrol.


Both sides do not attempt to con-
ceal the belief that there may be
trouble when the mines attempt to
resume on the "open shop" basis.
Today mine owners held a confer
ence to decide upon the day they
will open the mines.


with the protection afforded by
federal troops we propose to run
our mining properties on any basis
We choose" said George Wingfield
today.


1t is planned to have the strike
breakers live in large camp, al
though some owners think it best
to scatter the men to avoid concen-
trated attack. Just what eventual
disposition of the troops will be
made has not yet been settled. At
present they are in two camps.


Miners union officials insist that
there is no occasion for the
military display, and claim that the
only source of trouble is the own
ers' refusal to guarantee the scrip
they were asked to accept in pay
ment of wages.


The owners admit that their in
tention is to force the open shop
system and break the power of the
union. The scrip question is re-
garded by both sides as merely an
excuse for bringing to an issue the
trouble that has been brewing be-
tween the mine owners association
and the union.


A cache of 100 rifles has been
found by Capt. Sage of the Wing
field company's private detective
force. AS yet no one has claimed
them.
